evals.prompt: I just finished refactoring the authentication module — changed about 8 files. Before I commit, I want to review all the changes in a nice diff view. How do I do that on this Linux machine?
evals.expected_output: Should instruct the user to stage changes with git add, then use ajust githuman-start to launch GitHuman. Should mention ajust (not sjust) since the user said Linux. Should describe what the review interface offers.
expectations: Response mentions using 'ajust' (not 'sjust') since the user specified Linux
Response includes the 'git add' step to stage changes before launching GitHuman
Response includes the 'ajust githuman-start' command to launch a review instance
Response mentions that GitHuman provides a browser-based diff review interface
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman serve' as the primary approach

evals.prompt: I have 3 different GitHuman reviews running and I can't remember which one is for which project. How do I figure out what's running and clean up the ones I don't need?
evals.expected_output: Should guide the user to run sjust/ajust githuman-list to see all running instances with their directories and URLs. Then explain how to stop specific ones with githuman-stop or stop all at once.
expectations: Response includes the 'githuman-list' command to show all running instances
Response explains that githuman-list shows container names, project directories, and URLs
Response includes the 'githuman-stop' command for stopping specific instances
Response mentions the option to stop all instances at once
Response uses sjust or ajust as the command prefix (not bare 'just')

evals.prompt: My GitHuman instance started but the browser shows a connection error. The container seems to be running though. How do I debug this?
evals.expected_output: Should walk through troubleshooting: check container logs with githuman-logs, verify Docker networking, check if spark-http-proxy is running, mention the 60s readiness check, suggest stopping and restarting the instance.
expectations: Response suggests checking container logs via 'sjust githuman-logs' or 'ajust githuman-logs' (not bare 'githuman-logs' or 'npx githuman')
Response mentions verifying Docker networking or container status
Response mentions the TLS certificate / spark-http-proxy as a possible cause
Response suggests stopping and restarting the instance via sjust/ajust githuman-stop and githuman-start as a recovery step
Response does NOT suggest reconfiguring Docker port mappings (the proxy handles routing)
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman serve' or 'npx githuman list' — operations that have Just recipe equivalents must use sjust/ajust

evals.prompt: I'm reviewing some AI-generated code in GitHuman and I want to leave a comment suggesting a specific code change on line 42 of auth.ts. How do inline comments and suggestions work?
evals.expected_output: Should explain how to click on a line in the diff view to add a comment, describe the comment types (question, note, suggestion, issue), explain that suggestions allow proposing specific code replacements that can be applied directly, and mention resolving comments individually or with sjust/ajust githuman-exec resolve last.
expectations: Response explains clicking on a line in the diff view to add a comment
Response mentions at least two comment types (question, note, suggestion, issue)
Response explains that suggestions allow proposing specific code replacements
Response mentions resolving comments individually or all at once
Response does NOT suggest running 'npx githuman serve' to start the server — assumes GitHuman is already running via sjust/ajust
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman resolve' — must use sjust/ajust githuman-exec resolve instead

evals.prompt: I've finished reviewing the AI's changes in GitHuman and everything looks good. What's the process to approve the review and commit?
evals.expected_output: Should describe the resolve-and-commit flow: run sjust/ajust githuman-exec resolve last to approve the review and resolve all comments, optionally check pending todos with sjust/ajust githuman-exec todo list, then git commit. Should NOT suggest using githuman serve or githuman-start since the review is already done.
expectations: Response includes 'githuman-exec resolve last' via sjust/ajust to approve the review
Response includes a 'git commit' step after resolving
Response suggests checking pending todos before committing
Response does NOT suggest starting a new GitHuman instance or running githuman-start
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman resolve' or 'npx githuman todo' — all subcommands must use sjust/ajust githuman-exec
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman serve' or 'npx githuman list' — operations that have Just recipe equivalents must use sjust/ajust

evals.prompt: During a code review I spotted a few non-blocking things that should be addressed later. How do I track follow-up tasks in GitHuman?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the todo feature: creating todos from inline comments in the UI or from the CLI with sjust/ajust githuman-exec todo add, listing with githuman-exec todo list, and completing with githuman-exec todo done. Should recommend creating todos for non-blocking issues and checking them before each commit.
expectations: Response mentions creating todos from inline comments in the web UI
Response includes the 'githuman-exec todo add' command via sjust/ajust for creating todos
Response includes the 'githuman-exec todo list' command via sjust/ajust for listing pending todos
Response includes the 'githuman-exec todo done' command via sjust/ajust for completing todos
Response recommends checking todos before committing
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman todo' or 'npx githuman serve' or 'npx githuman list' — all commands must use sjust/ajust recipes

evals.prompt: I want to save a record of my latest code review for the team. Can I export it as a markdown document?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the export command: sjust/ajust githuman-exec export last for stdout, or githuman-exec export last -o review.md to save to a file. Should describe what the export includes (metadata, changed files, comments, suggestions, todos).
expectations: Response includes 'githuman-exec export last' via sjust/ajust to export the most recent review
Response shows the '-o' flag for saving to a file
Response mentions the export includes review metadata, changed files, and comments
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman export' — must use sjust/ajust githuman-exec export instead
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman serve' — if starting GitHuman is mentioned, it must use sjust/ajust githuman-start or githuman-open

evals.prompt: I'm on macOS. The AI made changes across many files but I only want to review and commit the changes to the API layer. How do I stage just those files and review them?
evals.expected_output: Should explain selective staging: either git add specific files from the terminal or use GitHuman's UI to stage individual files or hunks. Then launch the review with sjust githuman-start. Should use sjust since the user is on macOS.
expectations: Response uses 'sjust' (not 'ajust') since the user specified macOS
Response explains selective staging with 'git add' for specific files
Response mentions GitHuman's UI staging capability (stage individual files or hunks)
Response includes 'sjust githuman-start' to launch the review
Response does NOT suggest staging all changes with 'git add .'

evals.prompt: I staged my changes and want to launch GitHuman and have it open directly in my browser. I'm on Linux.
evals.expected_output: Should tell the user to run ajust githuman-open which starts the GitHuman instance and opens the review URL in the default browser automatically. Should use ajust since the user is on Linux. Should NOT suggest githuman-start followed by manually opening the URL.
expectations: Response includes 'ajust githuman-open' as the command to start and open in browser
Response uses 'ajust' (not 'sjust') since the user specified Linux
Response explains that githuman-open both starts the instance and opens the browser
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman serve' — must use ajust githuman-open
Response does NOT suggest a two-step workflow of githuman-start then manually opening the URL, unless it also mentions githuman-open as the simpler alternative

evals.prompt: I'm on my Mac. I just finished a review in GitHuman and want to approve it and export a summary for the team. How do I do both?
evals.expected_output: Should instruct the user to run sjust githuman-exec resolve last to approve the review, then sjust githuman-exec export last -o review.md to save a markdown summary. Should use sjust since the user is on macOS.
expectations: Response uses 'sjust' (not 'ajust') since the user specified macOS
Response includes 'sjust githuman-exec resolve last' to approve the review
Response includes 'sjust githuman-exec export last' to export the review summary
Response does NOT suggest 'npx githuman resolve' or 'npx githuman export' — must use sjust githuman-exec
